ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing First And Final Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-17-2018 Continued To Aug-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ruben Sundeen for University of California, San Francisco Foundation.
Giulio Accornero for Dennis Mullin also known as Daniel Andrews.
Nancy Lofdahl specially appearing for Debra Fox on behalf of Debra Dolch.

Ms. Lofdahl stated that she thinks the parties have worked out a solution, that this is a six year accounting, that it is ready to be filed, but it is not permissible, and that they are asking the Court to instead order a status petition which will include the six year accounting and request for fees, and order Mr. Rueppel to produce the documents that the parties are looking for with regard to the credit card bills. Mr. Sundeen stated that no one disputes the veracity of the accounting, that there are $70,000.00 plus going from the Estate to the Administrator's credit card, that a six year accounting resolves all of the issues in one lump sum and that it also shows that nothing happened in the first six years of administration. Mr. Sundeen also stated that he does not need the Court to review the accounting, that he and Mr. Accornero are happy to review it, that he thinks they can resolve the matter and that they need it to be part of the record. In addition, Mr. Sundeen stated that if they can't resolve it, then they could do two year accountings, that he has no records for the $70,000.00, that he does not know what the suspended Administrator's fees and the Attorney's fees are going to be, that those are the only two issues he sees requiring the Court's involvement and suggested continuing the status hearing so they could try and get an accounting for the $70,000.00 from Mr. Rueppel and get a petition for preliminary distribution filed. Mr. Accornero stated that he joined Mr. Sundeen's motion because he did not think the Administrator could produce an accounting, that he is willing to accept this accounting to be factual, and that he would like to see this accounting entered and be the basis for final distribution. Status on filing of the first report and account is continued to August 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that unless the order is amended, the order stands, that its point of view is Ms. Dolch is obligated to produce an accounting for the Court's approval that complies with Court rules and that unless something else is done, the Court will not change its order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-06-2015 Continued To Nov-17-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruben Sundeen for UCSF Foundation. Guilio Accornero for (heir) Dan Andrews. John Rueppel for (petitioners) Linda Conn Shocklie and Eddie Conn.

The Court stated that there are a couple of issues, that there is an ex parte petition to authorize the administrator to sell the property and to engage a real estate agent, that there is a response from UCSF that is not entirely clear, a pending petition to disregard the holographic will and stated that we will start with the request to list the property. Mr. Sundeen stated that his view on this will depend on the other issues. The Court stated that unless they are planning to submit today it will take awhile to resolve. Mr. Sundeen stated that he does not want to tie up the administration, that the only grounds to sell is a squatter, that there is no other evidence that the property is not secure, that they have tried to work out a compromise and that if it is to be sold the Foundation would like to do modest upgrades to obtain a better price. Mr. Accornero stated that his client just wants to maximize the return. Mr. Rueppel stated that it is to the benefit of the estate to sell, that the Foundation does not want the property, that the issue of squatters will not go away, that they understand the issue of the holographic will is pending and that the squatter disturbed personal property. Mr. Rueppel further stated that the broker says that there is not a great deal to be gained by making a modest improvement and that if UCSF believes it would help how long would it take. Mr. Accornero stated that the parties are talking settlement, that if the sale is made after distribution UCSF will not incur taxes but if it is sold now there will be capital gains taxes to the estate. Mr. Sundeen stated that there is a motion to vacate and set aside order taking place soon, that it does not mean the client will not appeal and that he thinks this should be continued to see how the motion plays out. The Court stated that it will grant the request to list the property for sale, that the court has appointed an administrator who is telling the court that it needs to be sold and is a drain on the estate, that it is assuming that the parties will talk further regarding their interests in the outcome, that the administrator will be able to use the agent they choose, that the court recognizes that Ms. Clemens is not a broker but that there is nothing to say that she is not competent and requested that Mr. Rueppel submit a corrected order. The Court addressed the issue of the holographic will. Mr. Sundeen stated that they have filed a motion to set aside the order but that it is going to be continued and that the next step would be a petition for lost will. The Court stated that it will need another status on this matter regarding the holographic will, that it will need to be later than the resolution of the motion and asked Mr. Accornero if his client is taking a stand on the petition. Mr. Accornero stated that they believe the Foundation has a right to submit the will and that they will be filing their papers this morning. The court stated that it expects the administrator to keep Mr. Sundeen informed as to what is happening. The petition for instructions was continued to November 17, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Order Due To Lack Of Notice And Related Order
Off Calendar Aug-12-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruben Sundeen for UCSF Foundation. John Rueppel for (personal representatives) Linda Conn-Shocklee and Eddie Conn. Guilio Accornero for Dennis Mullin.

The Court stated that the motion is premised on there being a default judgment but does not think that there is, that it is dealing with a proceeding with notice requirements, that the arguments in opposition are unpersuasive, that evidence supports the conclusion that there was not proper notice to the Foundation, that notice was sent to a non-existent address and pointed out that one of the orders being challenged was signed by a different Judicial Officer and that it cannot vacate that order. Mr. Sundeen stated that the parties have stipulated that Judge Busch could decide the ex parte order (and it was determined that it was a drop off ex parte as opposed to one with a hearing date), that the court has the ability to set it aside citing P.C. 7250, that the Peralta case is on point and that it is the list of potential heirs that triggers notice. The Court stated that only people with potential interest have a right to notice and that the argument would be that you have to start with the heirship petition/order. Mr. Sundeen stated that they do not have standing without the will being viable, that they will have to challenge the heirship petition but when and that opposing counsel can still file a demurrer. The Court stated that Peralta does not apply here, that they cannot file objections as they have no interest in the estate and that objections would not address the heirship order. Mr. Sundeen stated that they need to go backwards to where they were supposed to receive notice and that this is the first and proper step to get in. Mr. Accornero stated that they have presented the cases found, that they could not find a case regarding opposition and that P.C. 473.5 requires a pleading showing merit. Mr. Rueppel stated that the issue is whether the Foundation was entitled to notice. The Court stated that absent the heirship order any issue regarding heirship would need to go to everyone named in the will and had they told the court that the principle contingent beneficiary was not given notice it would not have issued the order. Mr. Rueppel stated that notice was sent but not to an effective address, that the Foundation has the possibility of probating the holographic will, that the heirship petition was filed because of the urgent need to sell the property and protect the assets and that it would be a hardship to the estate if the order is vacated. The Court stated that the petition was presented on the basis that all were given notice, that the court ruled on it and that this order is what is keeping him from participating in what is available. Mr. Rueppel stated that he is not sure it precludes the Foundations participation as it is not a ruling that the will is invalid. The Court stated that the courts order was premised on evidence before it and no objections and that the presumption was applied and could not be overcome. Mr. Rueppel stated that notice went out to 20 parties and that none filed objections. Mr. Sundeen stated that the property was specifically bequeathed to them under the will and that if they prevailed and the will was admitted they would have gotten the property. The Court granted the Motion to vacate and set aside order as well, stated that it will vacate the ex parte order without prejudice, that if it is presented again it will need to be a noticed ex parte and requested that Mr. Sundeen prepare a proposed order including that the ex parte order was vacated based on the stipulation from the parties.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Court Reporter: Angela Sinclair, CSR #13902, 415-362-4346 (Reported)
